events.name: Russia cuts electricity to Finland
events.description: Russia cut electricity exports to Finland (~10% of consumption). Fingrid described adjustment as 'not difficult'.

events.name: Russia cuts gas to Finland
events.description: Russia cut gas after Gasum refused ruble payments. ~75% of regional gas demand was Russian. Baltic states stopped Russian gas imports within two months.

events.name: Nord Stream sabotage
events.description: Underwater explosions ruptured 3 of 4 Nord Stream pipeline strings near Bornholm. Pipelines were not flowing but destruction was permanent. Largest human-caused methane release in history.

events.name: Balticconnector anchor damage
events.description: Balticconnector gas pipeline pressure dropped from 34.5 to 6 bar overnight. Newnew Polar Bear anchor caused 4m-wide drag marks. China acknowledged responsibility Aug 2024. Offline 6.5 months. Cost ~€35-40M. No gas shortages occurred.
events.source: Finnish NBI investigation, ENTSOG flow data

events.name: Estlink 2 technical fault (Jan 2024)
events.description: Estlink 2 fault in geotechnically demanding area on Estonian coast. Offline ~7.5 months. FI-EE capacity reduced from 1008 MW to 358 MW.

events.name: BCS East-West / C-Lion1 double cable strike
events.description: Yi Peng 3 severed BCS East-West (SE-LT telecom) and C-Lion1 (FI-DE telecom) within 18 hours. Ship turned off AIS for 7.5 hours. Also damaged NordBalt power cable (drag traces found). Both telecom cables restored ~10 days.

events.name: NordBalt suspected anchor damage
events.description: Swedish authorities found anchor drag traces on NordBalt power cable from Yi Peng 3 incident. Cable remained operational but with suspected damage.

events.name: Eagle S Estlink 2 severance + multi-cable damage
events.description: Eagle S (shadow fleet tanker, Cook Islands flag, Russian gasoline from Ust-Luga) dragged anchor ~90km. Severed Estlink 2 (650 MW) plus 4 telecom cables including C-Lion1 again. Finnish special forces boarded vessel before it reached Estlink 1. FI-EE capacity dropped 65% (1016→358 MW). Repair cost €50-60M.
events.source: Fingrid/Elering, Finnish Border Guard, Nexans repair reports

events.name: Latvia-Sweden telecom cable cut
events.description: Latvia-Sweden telecom cable severed. Later ruled accidental.

events.name: BRELL desynchronization
events.description: Baltic states disconnected from Russian BRELL grid and synchronized with Continental Europe via Poland. Operated in island mode ~24 hours. €1.2B EU-funded project. LitPol Link commercial capacity dropped from 500 to ~150 MW as it shifted to grid stability function.
events.source: Elering/Litgrid/AST joint announcement, ENTSO-E flow data

events.name: Estlink 1 outage (Sep 2025)
events.description: Estlink 1 out of service. Details limited from Elering news archive.

events.name: Kiisa battery fault — both Estlinks trip
events.description: 100 MW/200 MWh battery at Kiisa (near Tallinn) caused oscillations during grid connection testing, tripping both Estlinks. ~1000 MW lost (20% Baltic winter load). LitPol operated at double rated capacity to compensate. Reserve capacity deployed. Endogenous failure, not sabotage.
events.source: Elering statement, Energy Storage News Jan 2026
